refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing on single aspects of worksite area analysis without implying any implementation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate an analysis report or data set specifically describing the terrain characteristics of the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate safety and operational policy constraints into the terrain analysis
Provide an interface to share the generated worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently, which can then be used by other DPs or modules for feature extraction, policy integration, and sharing. This separation supports modularity and reduces coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that the terrain model can be generated without immediate integration of safety policies, which may require later coordination.
Risk that feature extraction and policy integration may introduce coupling if not carefully modularized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining separate DPs for feature extraction and policy integration to maintain independence and clarity in the design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functionalities, including terrain modeling and environmental data generation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle and other sources to build the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters related to terrain model generation tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends the generated digital terrain model data to other modules or external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is a core function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since data inputs come via Telemetry and outputs via Network Link.
Vehicle primarily provides raw data; implementing terrain modeling here would diffuse logic outside core processing modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and command; terrain modeling is outside its scope, causing high diffusion.
Safety Services is unrelated to terrain modeling, leading to very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP, as it centralizes environmental data processing and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate Telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
If terrain model data is needed by multiple modules, interface design must ensure efficient and consistent data sharing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain' by specifying a concrete method to analyze the terrain through digital modeling.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on timely and accurate Telemetry data input may affect model accuracy and cause indirect diffusion risk if data is delayed or inconsistent.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single IM boundary. Terrain modeling logic is centralized, minimizing diffusion across other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the generated digital terrain model can be validated against sensor data and must be made available to planning/execution functions as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is supported by the module's scope and containment scores, with well-defined interfaces for input (Telemetry, Tasking) and output (Network Link).

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate risks related to dependency on Telemetry data quality and timeliness to ensure model accuracy.
Design interfaces to efficiently share the terrain model data with other modules requiring it, to prevent diffusion and maintain consistency.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and containment scores. The DP directly satisfies the FR with a concrete method, the IM encapsulates the DP well with minimal diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although there are some risks related to telemetry data dependency, these are manageable and do not warrant further decomposition at this time. The high safety criticality suggests monitoring and mitigation rather than further decomposition to avoid complexity.

revise.why: To monitor and mitigate risks related to dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness, and to design efficient interfaces for sharing terrain model data to prevent diffusion and maintain consistency.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be available and accurate enough for terrain modeling.
The NGHE Solution module's high containment score indicates effective encapsulation.
Verification methods for the digital terrain model are reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input may affect model accuracy and cause indirect diffusion risk if data is delayed or inconsistent.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data quality and timeliness metrics.
Verification results comparing digital terrain model against sensor data.
Interface design documentation showing data sharing mechanisms and diffusion control.
